competition.narrative: Competing against SAP ECC (incumbent) and JAGGAER in a formal RFP (Oct 2026). SAP ECC is the primary competitor: Pladis UK runs iSupplier today (procurement only, no invoicing, no AI, 'really old platform' per Alessandro 2026-05-13), so SAP has deployment inertia and familiarity but is exposed on AI capability (no agentic negotiation), modern UX, and invoicing gaps. Alessandro rated Zycus 'top one' on 2026-05-13, signaling SAP ECC is vulnerable if Zycus can demonstrate the Anna ROI (£900k-£1.5M savings) and modern platform value in the business case. JAGGAER: mentioned as a competitor in the Oct RFP field (known insight from prior sweep). No buyer quote directly naming JAGGAER in this sweep's call notes, but the formal RFP structure (multi-vendor sourcing per Alessandro 2026-06-04) implies at least 2-3 vendors will bid alongside Zycus. Zycus wedge: Anna (Merlin AI agent for supplier negotiation and tail-spend automation) is the differentiation vs SAP ECC (no AI) and JAGGAER (AI capability weaker than Merlin per Zycus positioning). Alessandro requested the RFP primer to help shape tender questions for a 'modern agentic-AI source-to-pay platform', signaling Zycus can influence the RFP criteria to favor AI ROI and agentic automation (where SAP ECC and JAGGAER are weakest). How we win: (1) deliver the finalized business case with conservative £900k-£1.5M Anna savings claim before IT July submission, endorsed by EB/CFO; (2) position Anna negotiation agent and Merlin orchestration as the Phase 2 centerpiece in the RFP primer, shaping the tender to score AI capability heavily; (3) secure DM (Jennifer Parise) and EB active sponsorship before the Oct RFP so Zycus enters as the internally endorsed frontrunner rather than one bidder among several. Risk: without EB engagement and with the value questionnaire overdue, the business case may be incomplete or unconvincing for IT/CFO, and competitors (especially SAP ECC as familiar incumbent) may position on lower-risk deployment and integration during the RFP.

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is a phased IT-led CapEx approval followed by formal multi-vendor RFP sourcing, read from the call sequence and Alessandro's timeline. Phase 1 (in flight, at risk): Alessandro (Procurement Excellence, Evaluator) builds the business case with Zycus; IT leader (unnamed) submits CapEx request in July 2026; CFO/exec (unnamed) approves budget end-July. Phase 2 (post-budget): formal RFP released Oct 2026 (Alessandro, 2026-06-04 and 2026-05-13), multi-vendor evaluation including SAP ECC (incumbent) and JAGGAER, vendor selection by Q4 2026, contracting and 2027 UK deployment. Jennifer Parise (DM, his manager) has not joined any session; IT leader and CFO have not appeared. The approval chain per Alessandro: IT CapEx submission (July) → CFO/exec budget approval (end-July) → Procurement RFP release (Oct) → vendor selection and contracting (Q4 2026-Q1 2027). The gap: the IT and CFO decision-makers driving Phase 1 are unnamed and unengaged, so Zycus has no visibility into their evaluation lens, competing priorities, or readiness to approve the business case Alessandro is building. The Oct RFP will involve a formal evaluation committee (likely Procurement, IT, Finance, Ops); without DM/EB active sponsorship before the RFP, Zycus enters as one bidder among several rather than the incumbent-endorsed frontrunner.

north_star_verdict.headline: At Risk: buyer-owned deliverable 13 days overdue blocks July IT CapEx submission; EB/IT budget holder unmapped 183 days before close; single-threaded on Evaluator with no DM engagement across 7 touchpoints.

flags: EB/budget-holder engagement: Alessandro is the champion (Head of Procurement Excellence, Evaluator) but does not control the IT CapEx decision. He stated 'IT will submit the CapEx in July' and budget approval expected 'end of July,' but no IT leader, CFO, or executive sponsor has been named or engaged on any call. The business case is being built for an unnamed decision-maker. Map the IT/CFO budget sign-off authority now (ask Alessandro for the intro) and engage them directly before the July submission to validate the business case, surface objections early, and convert budget approval from a passive gate to an active sponsorship. Without EB validation, the July approval is a black box.

Activate the Merlin/Anna agentic-AI wedge as the quantified win story: Alessandro stated 'After Vienna, you become top one. What I've seen in terms of Merlin was quite impressive' (13 May) and accepted the Anna tail-spend ROI model (£30M at 3-5% savings = £900k-£1.5M annually, 5-6x platform cost payback). The Merlin negotiation agent for tail spend and the AI-assisted intake are the differentiated capabilities vs. the SAP ECC incumbent (which has none) and vs. competitors who will bid on guided-buy alone in the October RFP. Make the quantified AI savings (Anna ROI + Merlin intake cycle-time reduction + digital supplier collaboration efficiency) the centerpiece of the finalized business case (July delivery, once Alessandro returns the value questionnaire) and the RFP positioning. Ensure the RFP primer sent to Alessandro emphasizes agentic-AI evaluation criteria (autonomous negotiation, human-in-loop approval, AI benchmarking) so the October RFP scores Zycus's AI capabilities higher than non-AI or AI-lite competitors. The AI story is the wedge; quantify it, activate it in the business case, and embed it in the RFP questions.

Alessandro emphasized 'be super mindful of budget. If the budget is higher, you need to demonstrate the value' (13 May, quoting the UK MD's directive). Budget approval timing is end-July per Alessandro; any slip pushes the RFP release (Oct) and 2027 deployment at risk.

No Economic Buyer or executive sponsor identified or engaged. Alessandro (Evaluator, champion) does not control the IT CapEx budget decision (July submission, unnamed IT/CFO holder). Jennifer Parise (Decision Maker, his manager) invited to September event but has not attended any call or confirmed attendance. The business case is being built for an unknown IT/CFO decision-maker who has not validated the scope, ROI, or Zycus positioning. Without EB engagement before the July IT submission, the budget approval is a black box and the deal is at risk of silent objection or deprioritization.

Value questionnaire (due ~18 Jun, two weeks post-4 Jun call) is now ~8 days overdue as of 26 Jun. Next Step 22 Jun states 'waiting for him to come back on the value questions.' This blocks Zycus from finalizing the business case and commercial proposals for Alessandro to present to IT in July. If the questionnaire is delayed beyond early July, the IT CapEx submission timing (July, per Alessandro) is at risk, which cascades to budget approval (end-July), RFP release (Oct), and 2027 deployment. The overdue questionnaire is the single blocking item today.

Single-threaded on Alessandro (Evaluator). Only 2 buyer-side contact roles; Alessandro is the sole engaged buyer across all 7 touchpoints (27 Feb to 22 Jun). Jennifer Parise (DM, his manager) has not joined any call. The IT leader (CapEx budget submission authority), CFO/exec sponsor (budget sign-off), and procurement operations team (end users) are unnamed and unengaged. The October RFP will be a formal multi-vendor sourcing event; if the evaluation committee is unfamiliar with Zycus when competitors bid, Zycus loses the incumbent advantage despite being 'top one' pre-RFP. Ghost risk is high if Alessandro's advocacy is the only relationship and he cannot secure DM/EB active sponsorship before the RFP.

EB/budget-holder engagement: Alessandro is the champion (Head of Procurement Excellence, Evaluator) but does not control the IT CapEx decision. He stated 'IT will submit the CapEx in July' and budget approval expected 'end of July,' but no IT leader, CFO, or executive sponsor has been named or engaged on any call. The business case is being built for an unnamed decision-maker. Map the IT/CFO budget sign-off authority now (ask Alessandro for the intro) and engage them directly before the July submission to validate the business case, surface objections early, and convert budget approval from a passive gate to an active sponsorship. Without EB validation, the July approval is a black box.

Multi-thread upward and laterally before RFP release: Only 2 buyer-side contact roles; Alessandro is the sole engaged buyer across all 7 touchpoints (27 Feb to 22 Jun). Jennifer Parise (Director of Procurement UKI, Decision Maker, Alessandro's manager) was invited to the September Zycus event but has not attended any call or confirmed attendance. The procurement operations team (Alessandro's direct reports, the users of the future P2P platform) and the IT co-author of the RFP are unnamed and unengaged. Single-threading on an Evaluator is a ghost risk, especially with an October RFP that will bring competitors into a formal evaluation. Convert Jennifer's September invite to active participation (ask Alessandro to have her join a pre-RFP alignment call in July/Aug), and add the IT leader and 2-3 procurement operations team members as contact roles and call participants before the RFP to build multi-stakeholder buy-in and reduce the risk that the RFP evaluation committee is unfamiliar with Zycus when competitors bid.

Activate the Merlin/Anna agentic-AI wedge as the quantified win story: Alessandro stated 'After Vienna, you become top one. What I've seen in terms of Merlin was quite impressive' (13 May) and accepted the Anna tail-spend ROI model (£30M at 3-5% savings = £900k-£1.5M annually, 5-6x platform cost payback). The Merlin negotiation agent for tail spend and the AI-assisted intake are the differentiated capabilities vs. the SAP ECC incumbent (which has none) and vs. competitors who will bid on guided-buy alone in the October RFP. Make the quantified AI savings (Anna ROI + Merlin intake cycle-time reduction + digital supplier collaboration efficiency) the centerpiece of the finalized business case (July delivery, once Alessandro returns the value questionnaire) and the RFP positioning. Ensure the RFP primer sent to Alessandro emphasizes agentic-AI evaluation criteria (autonomous negotiation, human-in-loop approval, AI benchmarking) so the October RFP scores Zycus's AI capabilities higher than non-AI or AI-lite competitors. The AI story is the wedge; quantify it, activate it in the business case, and embed it in the RFP questions.
